claim: Standard LLMs lose context between chat sessions; without persistent memory, agents must be re-briefed on user preferences, goals, and past interactions every conversation
actionable: Implement long-term memory systems (like Mem0) to persist user-specific information across sessions rather than relying on context windows.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: Mem0 is an open-source Python library specifically designed for building agent long-term memory; it extracts and stores memories from conversations, segregated by user ID
actionable: For self-learning agents, integrate Mem0 via 'pip install mem0-ai'; configure it with your LLM and vector store backend for memory extraction and retrieval.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: Mem0 performs conflict resolution automatically: when similar memories exist, it deduplicates and updates rather than storing duplicates, keeping the knowledge base clean and relevant
actionable: When implementing long-term memory, use systems with built-in conflict resolution (like Mem0) to avoid redundant and contradictory memory accumulation.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: You don't need to maintain conversation history to have long-term memory; passing only the latest user message + retrieved memories from vector DB is sufficient for context
actionable: Design agents to retrieve relevant memories for the current query rather than maintaining full conversation history; this reduces token usage and improves performance.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: Mem0 uses LLMs to intelligently extract memories from user-agent interactions, then stores them as vectors; it rewrites queries to retrieve the most relevant memories when needed
actionable: In your memory pipeline, use an LLM to extract key facts from conversations (not just raw text) and rewrite queries intelligently for retrieval to maximize relevance.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: Supabase integration with Mem0 enables persistent vector storage for memories across agent restarts; connect via environment variables for the vector database backend
actionable: For production agents, configure Mem0 to use Supabase as its vector store backend rather than in-memory storage; use connection string environment variables.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: Using user IDs from authentication systems (Supabase Auth) with Mem0 ensures memories are segregated by user with zero risk of cross-user leakage
actionable: Extract user IDs from your authentication system (Supabase Auth, etc.) and pass them to Mem0 for all memory operations to guarantee per-user segregation.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time

claim: Long-term memory enables agents to build user-specific knowledge bases that make responses more personalized and relevant over time, mimicking human learning
actionable: Design agents to use memory not just for retrieval but to continuously refine responses based on accumulated user-specific context.
source_title: Build AI Agents that EVOLVE Over Time
